tax: Placed at family rank, Quartz-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a hard quartz-lattice body fed by silica seep. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a hard silicate body with its own growth habit.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a faceted glassy cluster throwing scattered glints. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — shed perfect crystals and glassy grooves — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Quartz-Kin occupies silica-rich vugs and quartz seams. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where silica seep nucleates a quartz lattice and recur with the seep re-saturates the vug, sustaining themselves on silica supersaturation.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: poses as a crystal cluster, grows blades to a stimulus. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: hard glassy body and acoustic resonance. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: shard-growth and light-refraction. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Quartz-Kin matters because the richest and most dangerous crystal to harvest. It lets survey reports sort hard quartz hazards from soft gypsum ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Quartz-Kin is the family-rank grouping of hard silicate crystalline elementals built on quartz lattices within the True Elementals. Its members share a hard glassy quartz body that rings and refracts, and divide below into genus groups by solid shard-growth versus hollow resonant geode form. The hardest-bodied of the crystalline elementals.
